articles.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5 with Advanced Reasoning Capabilities
articles.summary: OpenAI has announced the release of GPT-5, featuring significant improvements in multi-step reasoning, code generation, and multimodal understanding. The model demonstrates near-human performance on complex academic benchmarks.

articles.title: Google Announces Android 16 with AI-First Interface Redesign
articles.summary: Google has unveiled Android 16 at its latest developer conference, introducing a completely redesigned interface powered by on-device AI. The update brings smarter notifications, context-aware suggestions, and improved battery management.

articles.title: Critical Vulnerability Found in 12 Popular npm Packages
articles.summary: Security researchers have discovered a critical supply chain vulnerability affecting 12 widely-used npm packages with over 50 million weekly downloads. The flaw allows remote code execution in affected applications.

articles.title: Apple M5 Chip Benchmarks Leak Showing 40% Performance Gain
articles.summary: Leaked benchmark results for Apple's upcoming M5 chip reveal a substantial 40% improvement in single-core performance and 55% gains in multi-core tasks compared to the M4, likely using TSMC's 2nm process.

articles.title: Anthropic's Claude 4 Surpasses GPT-5 on Key Reasoning Benchmarks
articles.summary: Anthropic has released Claude 4, its latest AI model that outperforms GPT-5 on several important reasoning and safety benchmarks. The model introduces a new constitutional AI approach that significantly reduces harmful outputs.

articles.title: Massive DDoS Attack Targets Major European Banks
articles.summary: A coordinated DDoS attack has disrupted online banking services across several major European financial institutions. The attack, attributed to a hacktivist group, peaked at 3.5 Tbps making it one of the largest recorded.

articles.title: Microsoft Makes Copilot the Default Experience in Office 365
articles.summary: Microsoft has announced that Copilot AI will now be the default interface across all Office 365 applications. Users can expect AI-powered drafting, summarization, and data analysis built into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.

articles.title: Major Ransomware Attack Hits US Hospital Chain Affecting 200 Facilities
articles.summary: A ransomware attack on Ascension Health has disrupted operations at nearly 200 hospitals across the United States. Patient data including medical records and insurance information may have been compromised in the breach.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra Camera System Review: Best Smartphone Camera Yet
articles.summary: Early reviews of the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra praise its revolutionary 200MP main sensor with AI-enhanced night mode. The new periscope telephoto lens delivers 10x optical zoom with minimal quality loss.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's AlphaFold 4 Solves Protein-Drug Interaction Prediction
articles.summary: Google DeepMind has unveiled AlphaFold 4, which can now predict not just protein structures but how drugs will interact with them. This breakthrough could dramatically accelerate pharmaceutical research and drug discovery timelines.

articles.title: EU Fines Major Tech Company €13 Billion in Antitrust Ruling
articles.summary: The European Commission has imposed a record €13 billion fine on a leading technology company for anti-competitive practices in the digital advertising market. The ruling requires the company to divest its ad-tech business within 12 months.

articles.title: Active Zero-Day Exploit Targets Google Chrome Users Worldwide
articles.summary: Google has issued an emergency security update for Chrome after discovering an actively exploited zero-day vulnerability. The flaw allows remote code execution through specially crafted web pages and affects all Chrome versions prior to 128.0.6613.84.

articles.title: Meta Now Requires All AI-Generated Content to Be Labeled
articles.summary: Meta has announced new policies requiring all AI-generated content on Facebook, Instagram, and Threads to carry visible labels. The company is deploying detection tools to automatically identify and tag synthetic media.

articles.title: NVIDIA Announces Blackwell Ultra GPU with 2x AI Training Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA has unveiled its Blackwell Ultra architecture, promising double the AI training performance while reducing energy consumption by 30%. The new GPUs will be available to cloud providers starting Q4 2026.

articles.title: New Open-Source AI Model Outperforms GPT-4 on Multiple Tasks
articles.summary: A consortium of researchers has released an open-source language model that outperforms GPT-4 on coding, math, and reasoning benchmarks. The 70B parameter model is freely available under Apache 2.0 license.

articles.title: Tesla Issues Full Self-Driving Recall After 3 Accidents
articles.summary: NHTSA has ordered Tesla to recall 500,000 vehicles equipped with Full Self-Driving software after three reported accidents. The recall addresses issues with the system's handling of edge cases in urban driving environments.

articles.title: Sophisticated Phishing Campaign Targets Microsoft 365 Users
articles.summary: Security researchers have uncovered a highly sophisticated phishing campaign targeting Microsoft 365 users with realistic login page clones. The attack bypasses MFA through adversary-in-the-middle techniques and has compromised over 10,000 accounts.

articles.title: Apple Vision Pro 2 Leaked Specs Reveal Lighter Design and Lower Price
articles.summary: Leaked supply chain documents reveal Apple's Vision Pro 2 will be 30% lighter, feature improved eye tracking, and launch at $2,499 — $1,000 less than the original. The device reportedly uses a new M5 chip for enhanced spatial computing.

articles.title: Quantum Computing Breakthrough: First 1000-Qubit Processor Achieved
articles.summary: IBM has announced the first quantum processor exceeding 1000 qubits, a milestone that brings practical quantum computing closer to reality. The new Condor processor demonstrates error rates low enough for useful quantum advantage in chemistry simulations.

articles.title: New Deepfake Detection Tool Achieves 99.2% Accuracy
articles.summary: Researchers at Stanford have developed a deepfake detection tool that achieves 99.2% accuracy across all known deepfake generation methods. The tool analyzes subtle physiological signals like blood flow patterns invisible to the human eye.

articles.title: Major Data Breach Exposes 500 Million Hotel Guest Records
articles.summary: Marriott International has confirmed a data breach affecting 500 million guest records including names, addresses, payment card details, and loyalty program information. The breach exploited a vulnerability in a third-party cloud service.

articles.title: WordPress 7.0 Released with Built-in AI Content Generation
articles.summary: WordPress 7.0 introduces native AI content generation, a completely redesigned block editor, and automatic performance optimization. The release also adds built-in accessibility checking and enhanced security features for site administrators.

articles.title: Google Releases Gemini 3 with Real-Time Video Understanding
articles.summary: Google has launched Gemini 3, its most capable AI model yet, featuring real-time video understanding, enhanced code generation, and improved factual accuracy. The model integrates deeply across Google's product ecosystem.

articles.title: Linux Kernel 7.0 Released with Major Performance Improvements
articles.summary: Linus Torvalds has released Linux Kernel 7.0, featuring significant improvements to the I/O scheduler, memory management, and hardware support. The release includes native support for RISC-V boards and improved containerization performance.

articles.title: New AI Coding Assistant Automates 90% of Boilerplate Code
articles.summary: A startup has released an AI coding assistant that can automate up to 90% of boilerplate code generation, database migrations, and API endpoint creation. Early adopters report 3x faster development cycles.
